title: Tunisian Eggs Frittata
description: A flavorful, malty, and dense Tunisian dish perfect for a weeknight meal, made with free-range eggs and minimal ingredients.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a 12-inch non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Chop the onion and add it to the skillet, cooking for 3-4 minutes until softened.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, ground cumin, and paprika.
Add the chopped onion, flaked almonds, and 2 tablespoons of Malted Syrup to the egg mixture and stir to combine.
Pour the egg mixture into the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes, until the edges start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake at 375°F for 15-20 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the edges are golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle with flat-leaf parsley.
Let the frittata cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Cook time may vary depending on the size of the skillet and the number of eggs used.
